title: Epidemiology

jokes.text: Halloween... a time of vampires, hobgoblins, Draculas, boogens, and headless mice.
jokes.setup: Traditional Halloween creature list
jokes.punchline: Random nonsense words 'boogens' and 'headless mice'
jokes.explanation: Classic Community absurdist opening - mixing real Halloween creatures with nonsense creates mild surreal humor

dialogue.speaker: George Takei
dialogue.line: Boogens and headless mice? That's not a traditional Halloween creature list.

jokes.text: I'm George Takei. You know what? Let's just get to it.
jokes.setup: Celebrity narrator introduction
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandoning the pretense of formal narration
jokes.explanation: Meta humor about breaking narrative conventions, perfectly delivered in Takei's style

dialogue.speaker: George Takei
dialogue.line: I'm George Takei, and I'll be your narrator for this evening.

dialogue.speaker: George Takei
dialogue.line: Just kidding, I don't care about any of you.

jokes.text: Chef gaga never reveals her caterer. And don't try reading my p-p-p-poker face.
jokes.setup: Dean deflecting question about catering with mysterious persona
jokes.punchline: Lady Gaga reference with stuttered 'poker face' lyric
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean character moment - his theatrical personality mixed with pop culture obsession

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: You want to know about the catering? Po-po-po-poker face, po-po-poker face.

jokes.text: It's military rations from an army surplus store.
jokes.setup: Dean's elaborate Lady Gaga persona about catering secrecy
jokes.punchline: Bland reality delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff deflation of pretense - cutting through Dean's theatrics with mundane truth

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: The catering arrangements are classified. Lady Gaga doesn't reveal her secrets.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: It's just sandwiches, Dean.

jokes.text: I didn't read your poker face. You left the containers in the garbage.
jokes.setup: Implication that Jeff cleverly deduced the catering source
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was just sloppy evidence disposal
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and deflation - sets up Jeff as detective then reveals mundane method

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I know exactly where this catering came from. The napkins are from Señor Kevin's, the cups are from Boco Roco's, and the little toothpicks are from—

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: We just threw all the leftovers together.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: ...I was going to say Ming's.

jokes.text: When it's not playing an endless loop of Abba's greatest hits, it's playing what I think may be your personal voice memos.
jokes.setup: Mentioning Dean's iTunes problems
jokes.punchline: Revealing embarrassing personal details broadcast publicly
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean embarrassment setup - his technological incompetence creating social awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Dean, your iTunes is synced to the school's PA system.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: What? No, that's impossible.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Your entire playlist just played over every speaker in the building.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Oh my God, what was playing?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: 'Feelings' by Morris Albert on repeat. Followed by 'I'm Every Woman.'

jokes.text: Note to self... get oil changed, check Netflix for that movie where Greg Kinnear... Plays a ghost... no, an angel... and something called human centi...
jokes.setup: Dean's voice memos playing publicly
jokes.punchline: Awkward trail-off before saying 'Human Centipede'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe comedy - innocent Dean almost saying something horrible, perfectly cut off

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Note to self: stop watching that movie... the one where they... uh... you know what, never mind.

jokes.text: Man, I wonder how much effort you put into being accidentally handsome for a costume every year.
jokes.setup: Looking at Jeff in costume
jokes.punchline: Calling out Jeff's effortless attractiveness as calculated
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed meta-observation about Jeff's character trait - sharp and character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You realize that whole effortless thing is actually really calculated, right? You've probably spent like an hour in front of the mirror perfecting that look of not trying.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's not true.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You're literally posing right now.

jokes.text: Only half the effort you'll be putting into eating and drinking tonight.
jokes.setup: Abed's comment about Jeff's effortless handsomeness
jokes.punchline: Jeff turning it into an insult about Abed's social awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Solid Jeff comeback - defensive but clever, character-consistent

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You're so handsome, it's effortless for you.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Well, you're so socially awkward, it's effortless for you too.

jokes.text: Guys, Shirley's costume is once again unwittingly ambiguous. I don't know what she's supposed to be, but I do know she's not miss piggy. I repeat... she is not miss piggy.
jokes.setup: Jeff's PSA about Shirley's costume being ambiguous
jokes.punchline: Emphatic denial that creates the very comparison he's avoiding
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reverse psychology joke - trying not to say something while saying it repeatedly

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Shirley, your costume is ambiguous. It could be interpreted as offensive.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: What? What do you mean?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm not saying you look like a [offensive stereotype], because you don't. You absolutely do not look like a [offensive stereotype]. I would never say that. That's not what I'm thinking at all.

jokes.text: He's acting like the impression of him we do behind his back.
jokes.setup: Pierce acting erratically
jokes.punchline: Comparing his behavior to their mocking impression
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor about character dynamics - their behind-the-back impressions becoming reality

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Pierce, what is wrong with you today?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Nothing! I feel great!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: He's been like this all morning.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Dude, you're being super weird.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I'm not weird, you guys are weird!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: No, you're definitely being like... *does exaggerated impression* 'I'm Pierce, I'm acting all crazy!'

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: That's not what I sound like!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's exactly what you sound like right now.

jokes.text: I'm not a doctor. I'm a doctor.
jokes.setup: Someone saying they're not a doctor when Pierce needs help
jokes.punchline: Rich immediately contradicting them by being an actual doctor
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective contradiction setup/payoff - good economy of language

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I need a doctor!

dialogue.speaker: Rich
dialogue.line: I'm not a doctor.

dialogue.speaker: Rich
dialogue.line: Wait, actually I am a doctor.

jokes.text: Get away from those hotties!
jokes.setup: Pierce transformed by infection
jokes.punchline: His priorities remain consistent even when zombified
jokes.explanation: Character consistency joke - even as a zombie, Pierce is still a creepy old man

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Even as a zombie, I'm still thinking about my money and my stuff.

jokes.text: Well, I am wearing a $6,000 suit, and you spent 3 days making cardboard robot armor.
jokes.setup: Troy asking how Jeff attracts women so easily
jokes.punchline: Jeff's brutally honest assessment of their different approaches
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff moment - shallow but self-aware, contrasting expensive simplicity with Troy's earnest effort

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: How do you do it, man? How do you attract women so easily?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: It's simple, Troy. You see a woman you like, you walk up to her, and you tell her exactly what she wants to hear.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: That's it?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's it. You, on the other hand, walk up to a woman and tell her the truth about yourself, which apparently is a turn-off.

jokes.text: I'm saying I remind girls less of taking their little brothers to comic-con.
jokes.setup: Troy suggesting girls feel sorry for Jeff
jokes.punchline: Jeff's cutting comparison about Troy's nerd appeal
jokes.explanation: Sharp, specific insult that perfectly captures Jeff's superficial worldview and Troy's earnest nerdiness

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Oh, so girls feel sorry for me? That's sweet. You know what girls feel for you, Troy? Nothing. Absolutely nothing. You're like a computer that learned to talk.

jokes.text: Just been proven racist by the racist-prover.
jokes.setup: Jeff guessing wrong Asian figure skaters for Pierce's costume
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment of his mistake with made-up term
jokes.explanation: Clever self-aware humor about accidental racism with invented 'racist-prover' term

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Wait, who are you supposed to be?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I'm an Asian figure skater.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Oh! Nancy Kerrigan!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Oksana Baiul?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: No, I'm a different one.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm doing that thing where I make assumptions based on race. That's... that's not good. I'm being very un-woke right now. I'm being downright... asiophobic.

jokes.text: But our costumes go together, and if you're not in yours, I'm just a guy in a spandex suit and a bike helmet.
jokes.setup: Troy upset that Abed isn't wearing his matching costume
jokes.punchline: Describing how ridiculous he looks without the context
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor showing Troy's vulnerability and need for Abed's validation

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Abed, you can't just wear half the costume! You look ridiculous!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I'm making a statement about consumer culture and arbitrary matching.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You look like you got dressed in the dark at a Goodwill!

jokes.text: I don't need to know which Dracula I am to be a Dracula. Nerd.
jokes.setup: Abed correcting Troy about vampire vs Dracula
jokes.punchline: Troy's anti-intellectual stance and calling Abed a nerd
jokes.explanation: Character-driven humor showing Troy's insecurity about being seen as nerdy

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Dracula is a vampire, Abed. There's no difference.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Dracula is a specific vampire. Not all vampires are Dracula.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Well excuse me for not going to vampire college. Look, I don't need to know all the vampire rules and regulations. I just know Dracula sucks blood and he's dead and he's scary. That's vampire enough for me.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: It's not about rules, it's about—

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You know what? You're a nerd, Abed. A huge nerd. And I say that with love.

jokes.text: My name is Alex!
jokes.setup: Star-Burns being called by his nickname
jokes.punchline: His frustrated insistence on his real name
jokes.explanation: Classic running gag - Star-Burns' eternal frustration with his nickname

dialogue.speaker: Star-Burns
dialogue.line: It's Alex. My name is Alex. Why does everyone call me Star-Burns?

dialogue.speaker: Star-Burns
dialogue.line: My parents named me Alex. A-L-E-X. Not Star-Burns.

dialogue.speaker: Star-Burns
dialogue.line: I go by Alex now. Please, just call me Alex.

jokes.text: 'Classified Phoenix. If found, repeat key phrase... echo, tango, X-ray, nine, nine, seven.' Yeah. Sounds delicious.
jokes.setup: Reading ominous military classification label
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic 'sounds delicious' response to clearly dangerous food
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan humor - Jeff's sarcastic response to obviously dangerous military rations

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Sounds delicious.

jokes.text: Leonard, you better back that pumpkin ass up, or I will make a pie.
jokes.setup: Leonard being inappropriate with Shirley
jokes.punchline: Shirley's threat using pumpkin/pie wordplay
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Shirley line - combines her Christian values with sass, using Halloween pumpkin for clever threat

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Leonard, you need to stop this right now or so help me God, I will bake you into a pumpkin pie.

dialogue.speaker: Leonard
dialogue.line: Is that a threat?

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: No, Leonard. It's a promise.

jokes.text: 'Doc Potterywood,' remember? 'Cause we were... / rich, bigger fish.
jokes.setup: Rich trying to reference shared pottery class experience
jokes.punchline: Jeff's dismissive interruption prioritizing the crisis
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Jeff's priorities and Rich's social awkwardness. Economy is excellent.

dialogue.speaker: Rich
dialogue.line: Remember in pottery class when you said—

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Rich, there's a crisis happening right now. We can talk about pottery later.

jokes.text: Holy crap! Leonard's a zombie! Zombie attack!
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to coordinate orderly evacuation without panic
jokes.punchline: Troy immediately causing the exact panic Jeff wanted to avoid
jokes.explanation: Classic comedic timing - Jeff's plan immediately undermined by Troy's impulsiveness

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Okay everyone, remain calm. We need to evacuate in an orderly fashion. No pushing, no shoving. Single file line to the exits.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: FIRE! EVERYBODY OUT! THERE'S A FIRE! RUN FOR YOUR LIVES!

jokes.text: You punched a lady bee!
jokes.setup: Action sequence with infected people
jokes.punchline: Troy's concern about the gender and costume of who got punched
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy moment - focusing on wrong details during crisis, showing his innocent worldview

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. Did we just punch a girl in a dinosaur costume?

jokes.text: Two percent milk, eggs, deodorant, puppy chow... Okay, it's officially a waking nightmare.
jokes.setup: Dean's shopping list voice memo playing
jokes.punchline: Britta calling mundane grocery list a 'waking nightmare'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation humor - treating boring grocery list as worse than zombie outbreak

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: ...and organic free-range butter, non-GMO quinoa, and fair-trade coffee beans...

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: This is a waking nightmare.

jokes.text: It can't be the end of days. It's not 2012 yet. It's not the end of days.
jokes.setup: Discussion about zombie apocalypse
jokes.punchline: Troy's literal interpretation of 2012 end-times prophecy
jokes.explanation: Character-driven humor showing Troy's literal-minded approach to pop culture concepts

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're telling me that in 2012, the Mayans predicted the world would end?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's the theory, yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: But it's 2013 and we're all still here. So either the Mayans were wrong, or we're already dead and this is hell.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Troy, that's not how it works.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Nope, I'm convinced. We're zombies. That explains so much about Pierce.

jokes.text: That jacket is worth more than the island you were grown on, Chiquita M.D.
jokes.setup: Rich making fun of Jeff's jacket folding
jokes.punchline: Jeff's classist/racist comeback about Rich's background
jokes.explanation: Character-revealing humor showing Jeff's defensive classism and casual prejudice

dialogue.speaker: Rich
dialogue.line: Your jacket folding technique is terrible.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yeah, well at least I have a jacket. And a technique. Unlike some people whose backgrounds didn't allow for either.

jokes.text: 'Chiquita M.D.'... I like it.
jokes.setup: Jeff's insulting nickname
jokes.punchline: Rich finding the insult actually appealing
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion - Rich's positive reaction completely deflates Jeff's intended insult

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know what? You're a kiss-ass.

dialogue.speaker: Rich
dialogue.line: A kiss-ass? That's actually... kind of nice. I like that about myself.

jokes.text: Rich, this is a good time to let you know that I hate you.
jokes.setup: Rich being likeable despite Jeff's attempts to insult him
jokes.punchline: Jeff's honest admission during crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff character moment - his petty jealousy expressed with brutal honesty

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Look, I'll be honest with you. In a real crisis, you're the guy I'd want by my side.

jokes.text: That jacket is worth more than the island you were grown on, Chiquita M.D.
jokes.setup: Rich criticizing Jeff's priorities
jokes.punchline: Jeff's classist and racist insult combining Rich's banana costume with doctor title
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining costume with profession. Shows Jeff's worst impulses under stress.

dialogue.speaker: Rich
dialogue.line: You know what your problem is, Jeff? You don't have any real priorities.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Oh, that's rich coming from a guy dressed as a banana with a stethoscope. What are you, a doctor's visit to the tropics?

jokes.text: Why six hours? Are they hosting the Oscars?
jokes.setup: Army taking exactly six hours to arrive
jokes.punchline: Comparing to Oscar ceremony length
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about arbitrary military timeline with good cultural reference

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: The Army is taking exactly six hours to get here.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: That's longer than the Oscar ceremony.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yeah, well the Army has standards.

jokes.text: And the winner for best adapted Oscar burn, Jeff Winger for 'oh, snap! The man who went there.'
jokes.setup: Jeff making Oscar joke
jokes.punchline: Jeff immediately commenting on his own joke as if it won an Oscar
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor - Jeff's narcissism shown through self-congratulatory Oscar parody

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'd like to thank the Academy for this moment...

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That joke was brought to you by years of therapy and a drinking problem.

jokes.text: Troy, we have to rise to the occasion like Ripley and kick monster butt in our undies.
jokes.setup: Abed trying to motivate Troy for heroic action
jokes.punchline: Reference to Alien's underwear scene as inspiration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - using movie reference to motivate real action, showing his meta worldview

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You need to embrace your inner hero, Troy. Think of Alien. When Ripley faced that xenomorph in her underwear, she didn't back down. She fought.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: That's not what was happening in that scene.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: The point is, vulnerability can be strength.

jokes.text: I'm not Ripley, Abed. I'm a cool, sexy Dracula. I make love to ladies, and I survive.
jokes.setup: Abed's Ripley reference for heroic action
jokes.punchline: Troy rejecting hero role for his vampire persona
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Troy's insecurity about coolness overriding practical heroism

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Get away from her, you bitch!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's the line from Aliens.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I know, but I'm a vampire now. I can't say stuff like that.

jokes.text: I felt like maybe I was special. Special? You're not special. I'm special. I was bit ten minutes ago, and I'm... Fine.
jokes.setup: Rich explaining infection symptoms
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's been infected longer while saying he's fine
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and irony - Rich proving his point by being obviously not fine

dialogue.speaker: Rich
dialogue.line: I'm fine, I'm totally fine. Just a little cough, maybe a slight fever, but nothing serious.

dialogue.speaker: Rich
dialogue.line: Been like this for... what, three weeks now? Four? But I'm handling it great.

jokes.text: Rich, just so you know, I hate you less now. That's how much I hate your normal self.
jokes.setup: Rich becoming infected and less competent
jokes.punchline: Jeff's backhanded compliment revealing his deep dislike
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Jeff's petty honesty expressed as twisted logic during crisis

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know, Rich, I have to say, even infected and falling apart, you're still somehow less annoying than you usually are.

jokes.text: Oh, this is how I die... As Miss Piggy.
jokes.setup: Shirley about to be overwhelmed by zombies
jokes.punchline: Her final concern being the costume misidentification
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - Shirley's dying concern validates Jeff's earlier warning about costume confusion

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Wait, these aren't zombies, they're just people in grey makeup!

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: That's not even a zombie costume, that's just... that's just Kevin from accounting!

jokes.text: You're not Miss Piggy. You're Glenda the good witch.
jokes.setup: Shirley thinking she'll die as Miss Piggy
jokes.punchline: Pierce correctly identifying her costume in his final moment of clarity
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback resolution - Pierce's moment of clarity solving the costume mystery

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: I'm going to die as Miss Piggy.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You're not Miss Piggy. You're a big fat pig.

jokes.text: Just so you know, I've always loved Peggy Fleming. You're not racist.
jokes.setup: Pierce's earlier racist figure skater confusion
jokes.punchline: His dying words absolving Jeff of racism
jokes.explanation: Good callback - Pierce using his final moments to resolve earlier racist accusation

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Jeff, before I go... you're not a racist. You're just... you're just an idiot.

jokes.text: Oh, cool. A dark basement. I was just thinking we should be doing this in a dark basement.
jokes.setup: Group ending up in classic horror movie location
jokes.punchline: Troy's sarcastic commentary on horror tropes
jokes.explanation: Good genre-aware humor about horror movie clichés, though not particularly character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: So let me get this straight. We're in an abandoned mansion, it's midnight, and we just split up. What could possibly go wrong? Oh wait, I know this one from every horror movie ever made. We're all gonna die. Specifically, me first, because I'm the funny one, and apparently that's a death sentence in these situations.

jokes.text: You called me a nerd up there. What defines a nerd... committing to an awesome Halloween costume with your best friend? Is that what nerds do?
jokes.setup: Troy calling Abed a nerd earlier
jokes.punchline: Abed questioning if friendship and commitment make someone a nerd
jokes.explanation: Character-driven emotional moment disguised as humor - Abed's hurt about Troy's rejection

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're saying that friendship and commitment make you a nerd?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: No, that's not what I'm saying.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Because I'm pretty sure those are the things that make someone NOT a nerd.

jokes.text: I don't know, Abed, because I'm not a nerd, which is why I'm not taking part in this very conversation.
jokes.setup: Troy denying he's a nerd
jokes.punchline: Claiming he's not participating in the conversation while actively participating
jokes.explanation: Good self-contradicting humor showing Troy's denial and insecurity about his nerd identity

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I'm not a nerd! And I'm not even going to dignify this conversation with a response.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: That's right, I'm staying out of this.

jokes.text: What is up with that cat? Is someone throwing it?
jokes.setup: Repeated cat appearances in basement
jokes.punchline: Troy's theory that someone is throwing the same cat
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor about repeated visual gag - Troy's innocent explanation for impossible situation

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You know what I think? I think someone's throwing the same cat down here over and over again.

jokes.text: This is not pretend play time, save-the-world time. It's quarter past let's-get-the-hell-out-of-this-building time.
jokes.setup: Abed wanting to fix the thermostat
jokes.punchline: Troy's colorful time metaphor for abandoning heroics
jokes.explanation: Creative time metaphor showing Troy choosing survival over heroism, rejecting Abed's movie logic

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm done being a hero. I'm like a soup that's been left out on the counter. At first, I'm hot and everyone wants me. But the longer I sit there, the more I turn into a weird, crusty, room-temperature disappointment that nobody wants to touch.

jokes.text: That's my jacket! My jacket... you're stretching it! You're stretching it!
jokes.setup: Jeff being pulled by zombie while wearing expensive suit
jokes.punchline: His concern about the suit damage while being attacked
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff crisis priorities - more concerned about suit damage than zombie attack

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: No, no, no! This is a Tom Ford suit!

jokes.text: Troy, make me proud. Be the first black man to make it to the end.
jokes.setup: Abed about to sacrifice himself
jokes.punchline: Reference to horror movie trope about black characters dying first
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor about horror movie racial tropes, delivered with perfect Abed sincerity

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: In horror movies, the black guy always dies first. I'm not black, but I'm going to die anyway because I'm expendable. It's like I'm the black guy of white guys.

jokes.text: I love you. I know.
jokes.setup: Emotional goodbye between best friends
jokes.punchline: Star Wars Han Solo/Princess Leia reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect emotional/comedic beat using iconic Star Wars reference for Troy/Abed relationship

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I know.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I know you know. I've always known.

jokes.text: I'm gonna be a nerd.
jokes.setup: Troy deciding to go back and save everyone
jokes.punchline: His realization that heroism equals being a nerd
jokes.explanation: Perfect character arc moment - Troy accepting his nerd identity to do the right thing

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Wait, I'm realizing something. To be a hero, I have to do nerdy things like study maps, learn logistics, understand complex plans...

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Oh no. Oh no no no. Being a hero means being a nerd.

jokes.text: That's right. Prepare to meet the power of imagination.
jokes.setup: Troy returning to fight zombies
jokes.punchline: His attempt at heroic one-liner using childish phrase
jokes.explanation: Good character humor - Troy's innocent attempt at badass one-liner falls flat

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Hey zombies, you just got... Troy'd!

jokes.text: All right, I don't know why I thought this would work. Hey, get off me! Get off... you're so strong! God, I hate this! Stop using your mouth!
jokes.setup: Troy's heroic charge against zombies
jokes.punchline: Immediate realization his plan isn't working and complaining about zombie tactics
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of heroic moment - Troy's complaints about zombie fighting etiquette

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: This is it! Time to take them down! Come on!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait — this isn't working! Why won't they stay down?!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: That's not fair! They're not following the rules! Zombies aren't supposed to be this coordinated!

jokes.text: What big fists you have... In your face! Beam this up, Scotty. In your face!
jokes.setup: Troy fighting zombie Annie
jokes.punchline: Mixing fairy tale reference with Star Trek while fighting
jokes.explanation: Character humor showing Troy's scattered pop culture references during crisis

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Annie, you're like Sleeping Beauty, except you're a zombie!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: And I'm Captain Kirk, and this is my Prime Directive: to boldly go... away from you!

jokes.text: I've been bit, y'all. Dag. Congrats. You did what the zombies do.
jokes.setup: Troy getting infected after his heroic attempt
jokes.punchline: His casual reaction and congratulating the zombies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy reaction - treating zombie infection like a minor inconvenience with weird politeness

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Hey, congratulations you guys. You got me. You're very fast. You're very... bitey.

jokes.text: Jeff, still cool as a zombie.
jokes.setup: Jeff appearing as infected zombie
jokes.punchline: Troy noting Jeff maintains his cool demeanor even zombified
jokes.explanation: Great character observation - even as zombie, Jeff's vanity and coolness persist

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Even as a zombie, Jeff's still got that cool guy thing going on.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, practice makes perfect.
jokes.setup: Dean commenting on exactly six-hour response time
jokes.punchline: Officer implying they've done this zombie cleanup before
jokes.explanation: Dark implication about military zombie outbreak experience - suggests this happens regularly

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Six hours. That's an oddly specific response time.

dialogue.speaker: Military Officer
dialogue.line: Well, we've found that's how long it takes for the flesh to stop moving.

jokes.text: Let's dose these suckers.
jokes.setup: Military discovering survivors
jokes.punchline: Casual decision to drug/memory-wipe everyone
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about military cover-up procedures - treating memory erasure casually

dialogue.speaker: Military Officer
dialogue.line: We've discovered survivors. What should we do about them?

dialogue.speaker: Military Officer
dialogue.line: You know what, let's just drug them and wipe their memories.

jokes.text: Can't believe that somebody would mass-roofie an entire party. I can't believe it wasn't me.
jokes.setup: Pierce learning about mass drugging at party
jokes.punchline: His disappointment at not being the one who did it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce character moment - his creepy personality making him disappointed he wasn't the perpetrator

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Wait, so someone drugged the punch bowl? And it wasn't me?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I've been trying to do that for years.

jokes.text: I don't know why, but I'm kind of over zombies. Yeah, I don't feel like anything action-heavy.
jokes.setup: Choosing what movie to watch
jokes.punchline: Being tired of zombies after living through zombie outbreak
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-humor about genre fatigue after living the genre - classic Community self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: What about that zombie movie?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: No way. After what we went through, I'm done with zombies.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Come on, it's a classic!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Troy, we literally fought the undead. I'm not watching people pretend to do it for entertainment.

jokes.text: Hi, Kevin can't come to the phone. He's on a spaceship with me, George Takei. Please leave a message. You're welcome Kevins.
jokes.setup: Takei offering free voicemail message for people named Kevin
jokes.punchline: Bizarre specific offer involving spaceship scenario
jokes.explanation: Surreal Community-style tag - completely random celebrity favor for specific name group

dialogue.speaker: George Takei
dialogue.line: Hello, I'm George Takei, and I'm offering a free voicemail message for anyone named Kevin. When they call, they'll hear me say: 'Kevin, this is Captain Kirk. We need you on the bridge immediately. The dilithium crystals are failing, and you're the only one who can save the ship.'

jokes.text: Shirley and I totally did it!
jokes.setup: Chang leaving dramatic final message
jokes.punchline: His dying words are a lie about hooking up with Shirley
jokes.explanation: Classic Chang moment - using crisis to spread completely fabricated sex story

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Tell Shirley... tell her I always loved her. We had a moment together once. A beautiful moment.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: What? We did NOT!

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: She'll never know the truth.

jokes.text: Why did he call me?
jokes.setup: Chang leaving Troy a voicemail about hooking up with Shirley
jokes.punchline: Troy's confusion about why Chang would tell him this
jokes.explanation: Good button on Chang's lie - Troy's innocent confusion about being chosen as confidant

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Troy, it's me. I'm calling to tell you that Shirley and I hooked up.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Why would you call me to tell me that? Why would you do that?

tmdb_overview: Dean Pelton's army surplus party food sparks an outbreak of zombie-ism at the Greendale Halloween party.
insight: Halloween zombie premise sustains 60 jokes across 60 minutes with minimal setup fatigue.
